title: (CS)²AI Online™ Seminar: Fortifying Critical Functions using CIE
description:
*Register now to save yourself a seat!*
[https://events.zoom.us/ev/Asx3LT4tmNcZqKxjuEAGXIgd_8W2xGdggVtk_pn756NPUSFjyDJc~AljeJbVcVGC6V4p8cLtMEXHRaLjwTyhNaklb8cAg7CHbbolP9KNFwg6Mlj8daDhVg_cgDZqBZaJV-_2D_CJMssAnLg](https://events.zoom.us/ev/Asx3LT4tmNcZqKxjuEAGXIgd_8W2xGdggVtk_pn756NPUSFjyDJc~AljeJbVcVGC6V4p8cLtMEXHRaLjwTyhNaklb8cAg7CHbbolP9KNFwg6Mlj8daDhVg_cgDZqBZaJV-_2D_CJMssAnLg)

# **Design Out the Risk: The Cyber-Informed Engineering (CIE) Masterclass**

**Stop reacting to threats. Start engineering them out of existence.**
In an era where digital threats have physical consequences, traditional "bolt-on" security is no longer enough. We invite you to a session on **Cyber-Informed Engineering (CIE)**—the framework developed to integrate security directly into the engineering design lifecycle. (pre-cursor to a more intensive option at the Level Zero Conference).
This isn't just a lecture; it’s a toolkit handover. Whether you are managing microgrids, BESS, or complex gas compressor stations, you will walk away with the exact blueprints needed to bridge the gap between the control room and the boardroom.

***

## **The Agenda**

### **Part I: The Paradigm Shift**

* **Meet the Architects:** Introduction to the team leading the national transition toward CIE.
* **The CIE Core:** What is Cyber-Informed Engineering, and why is it the definitive answer to sophisticated infrastructure attacks?
* **Battle-Tested: The CIE Case Study:** A deep-dive post-mortem into how CIE principles transform vulnerable systems into resilient assets.

### **Part II: Strategic Integration**

* **From Engineering to the Boardroom:** How to connect CIE to **Enterprise Risk Management (ERM)**. Learn the adoption pathways that secure executive buy-in and funding.
* **The Implementation Roadmap:** A guided discussion on applying these frameworks to your current cybersecurity program without reinventing the wheel.

### **Part III: The Specialized Toolkit**

* **Sector-Specific Deep Dives:** Tailored tool demonstrations for:
* **Microgrids & BESS:** Securing the distributed edge.
* **Gas Compressor Stations:** Protecting high-consequence kinetic environments.
* **The Engineering Controls Database:** A tour of the universal tools and general application libraries available to you right now.
* **The Future—CMM for CIE:** An exclusive first look at the upcoming **Capability Maturity Model** to help you measure and scale your organization’s resilience.

***

## **What You’ll Carry Home**

By the end of this session, you won’t just have "information"—you’ll have **capability**:

* **Total Clarity:** A master-level understanding of CIE principles.
* **Day-One Application:** The ability to plug CIE directly into your existing security workflow.
* **The CIE Vault:** Full access to workbooks, tools, and the Engineering Controls Database.
* **Direct Access:** A directory of experts and resources for ongoing implementation support.

To learn all about Cyber Informed Engineering and its application to your ICS evnironment, **register now**:
[https://events.zoom.us/ev/Asx3LT4tmNcZqKxjuEAGXIgd_8W2xGdggVtk_pn756NPUSFjyDJc~AljeJbVcVGC6V4p8cLtMEXHRaLjwTyhNaklb8cAg7CHbbolP9KNFwg6Mlj8daDhVg_cgDZqBZaJV-_2D_CJMssAnLg](https://events.zoom.us/ev/Asx3LT4tmNcZqKxjuEAGXIgd_8W2xGdggVtk_pn756NPUSFjyDJc~AljeJbVcVGC6V4p8cLtMEXHRaLjwTyhNaklb8cAg7CHbbolP9KNFwg6Mlj8daDhVg_cgDZqBZaJV-_2D_CJMssAnLg)

title: Brian Knox: Stop Rewriting DAX: User-Defined Functions & the DAX Lib Ecosystem
description:
**IN PERSON MEETING!!!!!**
For years, DAX developers have lived with a dirty secret: copy-paste is our version control. Complex measures get duplicated across models, tweaked slightly, and inevitably drift out of sync. Calculation groups helped, but they came with their own complexity and limitations. This session takes you from zero to productive with User-Defined Functions. Introduced in September 2025, UDFs let you write reusable, parameterized functions in DAX for the first time and DAX Lib (daxlib.org) gives you 30+ community-built function packages to import and use immediately. This session walks through UDF fundamentals, live imports from DAX Lib, and practical examples of replacing duplicated measure logic with clean, maintainable function calls.
group: Hampton Roads SQL Server User Group

title: 10 Years A Kotlin Dev
description:
Kotlin has emerged as a compelling choice for both Android and backend development, offering a modern language with strong null-safety, concise syntax, and excellent interoperability with Java. In this meetup, we'll explore the key advantages that drive teams to adopt Kotlin—from improved developer productivity and safer Android apps to structured concurrency with coroutines on the backend. We'll highlight how Kotlin aligns with today's Android ecosystem, integrates with popular backend frameworks, and enables cleaner, more maintainable code across platforms. At the same time, adopting Kotlin is not without challenges. We'll take an honest look at the tradeoffs, including learning curve considerations, build performance, interoperability friction with legacy Java code, and operational concerns in backend systems. Through a retrospective lens, this talk will share practical lessons learned and guidance on when Kotlin is the right choice—and when it may not be—helping attendees make informed decisions for their Android and server-side projects.

This is a Hybrid event, you can also attend virtually using this link: https://discord.gg/nUSHDN2J2T
Once in the Discord Server, go to the voice chat named "Meeting Room"

Agenda:
6:30 PM: Doors open
7:00 PM: Main Event
8:00 PM Questions and Social Time

Whether you're coding, designing, experimenting with AI, launching a startup, or just curious about technology, you'll find friendly conversations and plenty of inspiration.

title: 🧜‍♀️ AICHR | Guardrails for Gen AI: From Policy to Practice
description:
Guardrails for Gen AI: From Policy to Practice
How to constrain AI systems without killing their value

Everyone uses the word "guardrails." Almost nobody means the same thing.

This co-presented session brings together AI strategy and cybersecurity expertise to break down what guardrails actually are, where they belong in your AI systems, and how to implement them without crippling the tools your teams rely on. We will cover the strategic layer — governance, risk classification, and authority boundaries — then dive into the technical layer with hands-on walkthroughs of real guardrails frameworks like NVIDIA NeMo Guardrails and Guardrails AI. Whether you are a business leader trying to deploy AI safely or a security practitioner trying to contain it, this session gives you a practical, layered approach you can take back to your organization immediately.

**What you'll learn**

* Why traditional security assumptions break down with AI systems
* The three layers of AI guardrails: governance, architecture, and implementation
* How to treat AI agents as high-risk identities using least privilege and Zero Trust principles
* Where guardrails actually get enforced — at system boundaries, not in prompts
* Real tools and frameworks for building guardrails into AI pipelines
* What guardrails can and cannot do — honest limitations and tradeoffs
* Hands-on homework: get started with Guardrails AI and NeMo Guardrails in 15 minutes

This is not about adding a system prompt and hoping for the best. It is about building AI systems that fail safely and stay auditable.

**Your speakers**

Andrew Stafford is a Fractional Chief AI Officer and Director of Technology and Global Alliances at The AI Collective. He works with executive teams to establish clear ownership of AI decisions, focus on real ROI, and avoid unnecessary risk, tool sprawl, and hype-driven strategy. Andrew brings 25 years in tech spanning Amazon, NASA, and five startups to the strategic and architectural side of AI security.

Ken is a security practitioner with ISSA Hampton Roads. He brings deep hands-on experience in security engineering and implementation, and will lead the technical deep dive into guardrails tooling, configuration, and real-world limitations.
Together, Andrew and Ken will walk you through a complete guardrails framework — from policy to architecture to working code — blending strategic thinking with practical implementation.

**Who should attend?**

* CISOs, security architects, and IT leaders responsible for AI risk
* Developers and engineers building or deploying AI agents and applications
* Product managers and business leaders evaluating AI tools for their teams
* Security practitioners curious about the AI threat model
* Anyone responsible for AI governance, compliance, or acceptable use policies
* Technical professionals who want hands-on experience with guardrails tools

No prior AI security experience required, but familiarity with basic AI concepts is helpful.

**Why this matters**

AI systems collapse data, logic, and control — and traditional security was never built for that. The real danger is not bad AI. It is trusting non-deterministic systems with authority. Organizations that get guardrails right will deploy AI faster and safer. Organizations that skip them will learn the hard way. This session is about making sure you are in the first group.

**FAQs**
Is there free parking? Yes. There is plenty of free parking available on the ECPI University campus.

Is this event free? Yes. Space is limited, so please RSVP and cancel if your plans change.

Will food or drinks be provided? Light snacks and drinks will be available.

**🤝 Organizers & partners**

**Organizers**

[TechArk](https://gotechark.com/)

**Bronze partners**

**🌍 ​Our parent org**
[The AI Collective](https://www.aicollective.com/) is a global non-profit community uniting 150,000+ pioneers – founders, researchers, operators, and investors – exploring the frontier of AI in major tech hubs worldwide. Through events, workshops, and community-led research, we empower the AI ecosystem to collaboratively steer AI’s future toward trust, openness, and human flourishing.

title: Black BRAND's The AI Meetup: Website Creation
description:
**Black BRAND's The AI Meetup: Website Creation**

Join **Black BRAND**, with support from **The AI Collective Hampton Roads**, for an engaging and hands-on **AI Meetup** where community innovators, entrepreneurs, and professionals come together to explore how artificial intelligence is transforming the way ideas become real digital products.

On **Monday, March 23 from 6:00 PM to 7:30 PM**, we will gather at **Black BRAND Headquarters (520 E Main St Suite 4, downtown Norfolk)** for an interactive session focused on practical AI tools that help turn ideas into functioning websites.

During the event, **Chris Toon** will guide attendees through the process of using modern AI tools to rapidly build and launch web projects. Participants will see how platforms like **Lovable, ChatGPT, and Claude** can help transform concepts into working websites with dramatically less time and technical friction.

Members of **The AI Collective Hampton Roads** will also be present to support the session, answer questions, and help attendees better understand how these tools can be used for business ideas, creative projects, and organizational growth.

This meetup is ideal for **entrepreneurs, creatives, builders, professionals, and curious learners** who want to explore how AI can accelerate innovation and open new opportunities.

Attendees will have the opportunity to connect with others in the community, participate in interactive discussion, and walk away with practical insight into how AI tools can support personal, professional, and organizational growth.

**Important: Registration Required**
Registration through the event link below is **100% required**.

Anyone who has not registered in advance **may be turned away at the door**, as space is limited and attendance must be tracked through the registration system.

**Register here:**
https://app.glueup.com/event/black-brand-ai-meetup-171918/

**Location**
**Black BRAND Headquarters**
520 E Main St Suite 4
Norfolk, VA 23510

**Parking**
The closest parking option is **Fountain Park Garage (500 E Main St)**, located just steps from the venue.

The **City of Norfolk offers the first 2 hours of parking free in participating downtown city garages**, so most attendees will be able to park for free during the event.

Other nearby garages within a short walking distance include **Main Street Garage (235 E Main St)** and **Bank Street Garage (300 Bank St)**.

The Information Systems Security Association of Hampton Roads (ISSA-HR) is setting up social events throughout Hampton Roads to give Cyber Security Professionals and those interested in the subject a place to: Get Together, Share Stories, Learn Information, Meet New People.

title: 🧜‍♀️ AICHR | Connections & (grocery) carts
description:
Meet people and pick up your weekly groceries.

🤝 **A casual networking hangout in the grocery store cafe**
Chats, connections, & grocery carts is a relaxed, social-first networking event for curious, friendly people across Hampton Roads who enjoy good conversation and knocking out life errands at the same time.

This is not a lecture.
This is not a pitch night.
And you definitely do not need to “prepare” anything.

It is simply a low-pressure space to meet new people, have real conversations, and casually connect while wandering the aisles and picking up your weekly groceries.

Whether you are deeply into AI and automation, adjacent to tech and creativity, or just someone who enjoys thoughtful conversations with interesting humans, you belong here.

✨ **What to expect**
\- Casual\, open networking with a mix of builders\, creatives\, technologists\, founders\, students\, and professionals
\- Easy\, organic conversations while shopping at your own pace
\- Light\, optional conversation prompts to help break the ice if you want them
\- No presentations\, no panels\, no pressure to “network correctly”
\- A friendly\, welcoming environment for all backgrounds and experience levels
\- Come as you are\. Grab a cart or a basket\. Pick up dinner for the week\. \- \- Meet a few new people along the way\.

👥 **Who should attend**
Chats, Connections, & Grocery Carts is for:

People who enjoy meeting others without forced networking vibes
Builders, creatives, technologists, and curious minds
AI, automation, no-code, and tech-adjacent folks
Students and career switchers exploring what is next
Founders, consultants, operators, and professionals
Anyone who likes good conversation and low-stakes socializing

If you enjoy thoughtful chats and meeting people in unexpected places, you will fit right in.

🏪 **Why a grocery store**
You were probably going to the store anyway.

Hosting this event in a grocery store keeps things human, practical, and approachable. It removes the pressure of traditional networking and turns it into something simple, efficient, and a little fun.

Two errands. One cart.

💡 **Why this event exists**
Community grows best in relaxed, everyday spaces.

Chats, Connections, & Grocery Carts exists to create an easy, human-centered way for people to connect without titles, talks, or expectations. It is about building relationships, sharing ideas naturally, and strengthening the local community one conversation at a time.

Come say hi. Leave with groceries. Make a few new connections along the way.

**ℹ️ Other bits of info & FAQs**

* This is a free community event
* Drinks or food are available for purchase depending on the venue
* Photos or videos may be taken for community and marketing purposes
* Come solo or bring a friend
* ​​​​Is there free parking in the area? Yes. Wegman's has got lots of spots available in their many parking lots.
* ​​​Is this free? Yes, but space is limited. Make sure to RSVP.

title: jj - version control for the modern dev
description:
Join us to hear from Cleo on an up and coming version control system:

*"As a young developer i’m very prone to using the latest technology; one of the most recent pieces of tech i’ve picked up is the jujutsu VCS. jujutsu, or jj is a modern version control system designed to make version history easier. Since it uses git as a backend, it’s super simple and quick to replace your existing git workflow with [jujutsu!](https://www.jj-vcs.dev/latest/)"*

All skill levels welcome! The [757Dev Community](757dev.org) means a lot to me, and I thank each of you for your support. 🖖

As always, HUGE thanks to Assembly for hosting. Learn more about the amenities and community of Assembly at [https://www.assemblynfk.com/](https://www.assemblynfk.com/)

Street parking is free after 6 pm. From 6:30-7:00 pm, we will have someone at the door to let you in. After 7, message the #javascript channel on 757Dev Slack. We meet in the Fur Room on the 4th floor.

title: (CS)²AI Online™ Symposium: ICS/OT Cybersecurity in the Manufacturing Sector
description:
Register now: https://events.zoom.us/ev/AmMfHwK3X4ldZMXmp0Cz2cJnZkIEUck3-0YUlRrwOJcFfN38eNaW\~AqEkrMaoscLWK-LtBdz5alf-KHHX6NTpJ2q8lR3hOkZ191-0EUPcupcvmaVVu7buKlRWQccJa7SMiit3RiEIUpvWjw

## **Shielding the Shop Floor: A Symposium on ICS/OT Cybersecurity**

In an era where a single line of code can halt a production line across the globe, resilience isn't just a goal—it’s a requirement for survival. Join us for an intensive, high-impact symposium dedicated to the unique challenges of **ICS/OT** cybersecurity within the manufacturing sector.

***

### Why Attend?

The integration of IT and OT has opened unprecedented doors for efficiency, but it has also expanded the attack surface for bad actors. This symposium brings together frontline defenders, policy experts, and industry leaders to move beyond theoretical security and into **battle-tested resilience.**

### Key Sessions

* **Safeguarding Manufacturing Business Continuity in the Face of Disaster**
* *The Strategy:* Learn how to architect "fail-functional" systems that maintain core operations even during an active breach.
* *The Focus:* Bridging the gap between rapid incident response and long-term recovery to ensure your bottom line remains protected.
* **Case Study Estonia: Resilience in the Face of Cyber Warfare**
* *The Deep Dive:* Analyze the real-world tactics used by one of the world's most digitally advanced nations to repel nation-state cyber aggression.
* *The Takeaway:* Actionable intelligence on how manufacturing hubs can adopt a "Digital Defense" posture modeled after Estonian infrastructure resilience.

***

### Event Details

**Date** April 1, 2026
**Location** All Online
**Target Audience** CISOs, Plant Managers, OT Engineers, and Risk Officers
**Engagement** Live "Deep Dive." Ask the speakers and panelists questions live

> **"In the manufacturing world, uptime is the only metric that matters. Cybersecurity is no longer an IT expense; it is the insurance policy for your physical output."**

***

### Secure Your Spot

Don't wait for a system alert to start thinking about defense. Join your peers in defining the future of industrial safety.
**[Download the Full Agenda PDF]**
Would you like me to draft a series of promotional social media posts for LinkedIn and X to go along with this listing?

Register now: https://events.zoom.us/ev/AmMfHwK3X4ldZMXmp0Cz2cJnZkIEUck3-0YUlRrwOJcFfN38eNaW\~AqEkrMaoscLWK-LtBdz5alf-KHHX6NTpJ2q8lR3hOkZ191-0EUPcupcvmaVVu7buKlRWQccJa7SMiit3RiEIUpvWjw

title: Late Night Builders
description:
Can't sleep? Up late tinkering on a project? Or can't make the usual meetups till the kids are in bed?

Late Night Builders is a monthly hangout for builders, tinkerers, and the generally curious. Developers, designers, AI enthusiasts, entrepreneurs, hobbyists — if you make things or want to start, you'll fit in.

Bring something you're working on, pick someone's brain, or just show up for cheap wings and good conversation. No agenda, no presentations, no networking cringe.

Anyone is welcome at any experience level. Bring your laptop, bring a friend, or just bring yourself.

description:

title: VBMBC + The HIVE: Spring Small Business Roundtable
description:
**Working Smarter, Not Harder: Scaling Your Business in the Digital World**

​Running a small business today means wearing many hats. The good news is you do not have to do everything the hard way.
​Join the **Virginia Beach** **Minority Business Council** and **The HIVE Small Business Resource Center** for an interactive session designed to help entrepreneurs understand how AI and modern digital tools can simplify operations, save time, and support sustainable growth.
​This session will introduce practical ways small business owners can use AI today. You will learn how automation and digital tools can reduce repetitive work, improve decision making, and help you focus more energy on growing your business.

​**Required registration**
​Registration is required to attend this event.

​Please sign up here:
https://www.eventbrite.com/e/2026-mbc-spring-small-business-roundtable-tickets-1981794826675)

​Attendees who have not registered may be turned away at the event if capacity is reached.

​**What you will learn**
​• What AI actually means for small businesses today
​• Practical digital tools you can begin using immediately
​• How automation can reduce manual work and free up your time
​• How to take advantage of resources already available to entrepreneurs in Hampton Roads
​
Whether you are just beginning to explore digital tools or looking for smarter ways to scale your business, this event focuses on practical strategies that work in today’s fast moving business environment.

​**Featured speaker**
​**Erin Marie**
​Co-Founder & Director @ The AI Collective Hampton Roads
​AI Modernization\, Automation & Strategy Consultant \| Former Microsoft Technical Program Manager

​Erin Marie is a co-founder of **AI Collective Hampton Roads**, a regional community that brings together builders, leaders, and practitioners to explore practical and responsible applications of AI. Through workshops, hackathons, and community events, the organization helps people learn how to apply AI in ways that are useful, accessible, and human centered.
​
Prior to this work, Erin spent five years at Microsoft as a Technical Program Manager leading modernization efforts for the Windows servicing pipeline. Her work focused on system design, distributed computing, and helping organizations modernize workflows through thoughtful adoption of automation and AI.
​She is known for helping technical and non technical teams work together effectively while building systems that support both business outcomes and the people behind them.

**Partnership**
​This event is hosted by **Virginia Beach Minority Business Council & The HIVE Small Business Resource Center of Virginia Beach**, which provides education, mentoring, and resources to help entrepreneurs launch and grow successful businesses.

**Parking**
​Parking is available in the **Red Garage**. Enter on Market Street, walk around the building to **140 Independence Blvd**, and enter through the front entrance.

title: Cybersecurity - Chesapeake Weekend Afternoon Networking (WAN)
description:
**Attention Information Security and Cybersecurity Professionals!**
Are you looking to meet new people who share your interests? Whether you're thinking about entering cybersecurity, have recently entered the field, or are a seasoned professional, we welcome all to join us for an evening of networking, learning, and fun.
**Why Attend?**
Enjoy a night out at the cozy and welcoming **Neat Bird** in Chesapeake, where you can connect with like-minded individuals. We love bringing people together to share insights and build lasting connections. Join us for some beers—or not, if that's your preference. Our goal is to help you forge meaningful relationships in the cybersecurity community.
**About ISSA-HR**
The Information Systems Security Association of Hampton Roads (ISSA-HR) hosts social events throughout the region, providing a space for cybersecurity professionals and enthusiasts to get together, share stories, learn new information, and meet new people. With years of cybersecurity expertise, ISSA-HR is committed to promoting the industry and nurturing the next generation of cybersecurity professionals.

**RSVP now and bring a friend!** We look forward to seeing you there!

**Location:** Neat Bird, 565 Belaire Ave, Chesapeake, VA 23320
Time: **6:00 - 8:00 PM**
group: Information Systems Security Association ISSA Hampton Roads

title: Quantum Computing with Q# and Azure Quantum
description:
**Quantum Computing with Q# and Azure Quantum**

Quantum Computing is a rising field in Computer Science that uses the effects of Quantum Mechanics to achieve computations that are very difficult to do or simply impossible to do with regular computer technology. It can be used to simulate complex molecules for research, find optimal solutions for complex datasets, or could eventually break RSA encryption with sufficiently powerful quantum computers. To enter the Quantum Computing field, Microsoft created Q#, a domain specific language for performing quantum computations and Azure Quantum, an Azure service that provides cloud access to quantum simulators and selected real quantum computers.

**Schedule**

* 5:45 PM — Doors open
* 6:00 PM — Food, drinks, and networking
* 6:30 PM — Presentation begins

**Location**
Marathon Consulting (2nd Floor)
Plenty of free parking is available behind the building. If you're arriving later, the doors may be locked—check the sign on the door for instructions on how to get in.

**Details**
Food and drinks will be provided. This event is free and open to all skill levels.

title: Level Zero OT Cyber Security Conference
description:
Meetup Members get 10% off!
Use code: **LZMEETUP10**
Atlanta, GA April 20 - 22
[www.LevelZeroConference.com](https://levelzeroconference.com/)

## **Attend The Level Zero Conference**

The Level Zero Conference brings experts from across industries together to address the complex challenges of securing operational technology. Our multidisciplinary approach integrates engineering, cyber security, and risk management to safeguard critical infrastructure from level zero to the cloud.

**Level Zero is designed for professionals responsible for the complex task of protecting their critical infrastructure from catastrophic failures.**

**Why Attend Level Zero?**

#### ***Bridge the IT-OT Divide***

Gain insights from both IT and OT perspectives to develop comprehensive security strategies

#### ***Expert-Led Sessions***

Learn from 60+ first hand authorities on OT threats, defenses, and compliance

#### ***Hands-On Experience***

Participate in the ICS Village, tabletop exercises, and interactive workshops

## **Level Zero Delivers.**

IT and OT have traditionally operated in silos, with each team lacking a critical understanding of the other. The Level Zero Conference bridges this divide by bringing together experts from OT and IT – engineering, operations, and cyber security.
Fostering collaboration and cross-disciplinary understanding are both essential for the protection of critical infrastructure. Through the exchange of ideas and expertise, Level Zero empowers professionals from different domains to develop a deeper understanding of both IT and OT environments enabling the creation of infrastructure that is integrated and resilient to evolving threats.

## **Who Should Attend?**

**Level Zero Conference is for individuals responsible for building, maintaining, and defending ICS/OT environments:**

#### **Engineers**

responsible for addressing cyber threats to physical operations, looking for effective ways to improve the maturity of their security program.

#### **Executives**

managing cyber security budgets and physical operations, wanting to better understand real threats and ways to build a successful cyber security program.

#### **Experts**

wanting to learn from other experts and catch up with each other.

#### **Practitioners**

needing contact with peers facing similar challenges and wanting to learn from experts.

#### **Compliance managers**

looking to reduce risks and lower costs.

#### **Enterprise security**

responsible for securing physical operations, wanting to get more insights into the threats of the cyber world.
![](https://levelzeroconference.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/10/it-ot-workers-1000x1000.webp)

## **Be Part of Securing Our Critical Infrastructure**

Level Zero Conference is where the future of OT cyber security is shaped. Don’t miss this opportunity to contribute to and learn from the industry’s leading minds.

#### **Venue**

***

##### Georgia Tech Exhibition Hall

Physical Address (for walking to facility)
460 Fourth St. N.W. Atlanta, GA 30332

Hosted by: Control System Cyber Security Association Internatioal
group: Hampton Roads Cyber Security for Control Systems

title: Geeks at a Bar- Peninsula Happy Hour
description:
Do you work with technology or for a tech company? Do you speak geek? Come network with other technology industry workers for a casual, conversational happy hour.

This meetup is loosely organized, but very consistently scheduled. If you want to meet up somewhere else, share it in the #peninsula channel at 757dev.org Slack, or in the comments.

title: Cybersecurity Social/Happy Hour meetup for networking, meeting new people
description:
Information Security and Cybersecurity professionals, come out and meet some new people. Have a night out on the town. Meet new people with similar interests. We love connecting with people who have similar interests. Whether you thinking about entering cybersecurity, recently entered, or seasoned, we welcome all to come out and drink some beers with us, or not drink with us. We want to meet you, to meet new people. We had enough of the virtual world with covid. Lets get back out and start meeting people again.

The Information Systems Security Association of Hampton Roads (ISSA-HR) is setting up social events throughout Hampton Roads to give Cyber Security Professionals and those interested in the subject a place to: Get Together, Share Stories, Learn Information, Meet New People.
I﻿SSA-HR has years of Cyber Security knowledge that we are happy to give. Our organization is here to promote Cyber Security and bring up the next generation of Cyber Security Professionals.

Location: **The Casual Pint**: 3380 Princess Anne Rd, Ste 111, Virginia Beach, VA 23456
Time: **6:00 - 8:00 PM**
group: Information Systems Security Association ISSA Hampton Roads

title: John Sterrett- Advanced Data Protection Strategies with Azure SQL
description:
**IN PERSON MEETING!!!!! (PLEASE NOTE DATE CHANGE FOR THIS MEETING ONLY)**
Organizations handle increasing amounts of growth with their sensitive data. Data engineers must protect their data from external attackers, application users, and even privileged employees like database administrators.

This session will teach you how to protect your data while maintaining functionality and performance. You will leave this session knowing how and why column-level encryption, row-level security, and auditing are keys to securing your data.
group: Hampton Roads SQL Server User Group

title: 🧜‍♀️ AICHR | Connections & (grocery) carts
description:
Meet people and pick up your weekly groceries.

🤝 **A casual networking hangout in the grocery store cafe**
Chats, connections, & grocery carts is a relaxed, social-first networking event for curious, friendly people across Hampton Roads who enjoy good conversation and knocking out life errands at the same time.

This is not a lecture.
This is not a pitch night.
And you definitely do not need to “prepare” anything.

It is simply a low-pressure space to meet new people, have real conversations, and casually connect while wandering the aisles and picking up your weekly groceries.

Whether you are deeply into AI and automation, adjacent to tech and creativity, or just someone who enjoys thoughtful conversations with interesting humans, you belong here.

✨ **What to expect**
\- Casual\, open networking with a mix of builders\, creatives\, technologists\, founders\, students\, and professionals
\- Easy\, organic conversations while shopping at your own pace
\- Light\, optional conversation prompts to help break the ice if you want them
\- No presentations\, no panels\, no pressure to “network correctly”
\- A friendly\, welcoming environment for all backgrounds and experience levels
\- Come as you are\. Grab a cart or a basket\. Pick up dinner for the week\. \- \- Meet a few new people along the way\.

👥 **Who should attend**
Chats, Connections, & Grocery Carts is for:

People who enjoy meeting others without forced networking vibes
Builders, creatives, technologists, and curious minds
AI, automation, no-code, and tech-adjacent folks
Students and career switchers exploring what is next
Founders, consultants, operators, and professionals
Anyone who likes good conversation and low-stakes socializing

If you enjoy thoughtful chats and meeting people in unexpected places, you will fit right in.

🏪 **Why a grocery store**
You were probably going to the store anyway.

Hosting this event in a grocery store keeps things human, practical, and approachable. It removes the pressure of traditional networking and turns it into something simple, efficient, and a little fun.

Two errands. One cart.

💡 **Why this event exists**
Community grows best in relaxed, everyday spaces.

Chats, Connections, & Grocery Carts exists to create an easy, human-centered way for people to connect without titles, talks, or expectations. It is about building relationships, sharing ideas naturally, and strengthening the local community one conversation at a time.

Come say hi. Leave with groceries. Make a few new connections along the way.

**ℹ️ Other bits of info & FAQs**

* This is a free community event
* Drinks or food are available for purchase depending on the venue
* Photos or videos may be taken for community and marketing purposes
* Come solo or bring a friend
* ​​​Is there free parking in the area? Yes. They’ve got lots of spots available in their parking lot.
* ​​​Is this free? Yes, but space is limited. Make sure to RSVP.

**🤝 Organizers & partners**

**Organizers**
[The AI Collective Hampton Roads](https://linktr.ee/aicollectivehr)

**​Platinum partners**
[TechArk](https://gotechark.com/)
[Regent University](https://www.regent.edu/)
[ECPI University](https://www.ecpi.edu/)
[Hampton Roads Chamber Foundation](https://hrchamber.com/)

**Bronze partners**
[Assembly](https://www.assemblynfk.com/)
[757 Collab](https://757collab.org/)

**🌍 ​Our parent org**
[The AI Collective](https://www.aicollective.com/) is a global non-profit community uniting 150,000+ pioneers – founders, researchers, operators, and investors – exploring the frontier of AI in major tech hubs worldwide. Through events, workshops, and community-led research, we empower the AI ecosystem to collaboratively steer AI’s future toward trust, openness, and human flourishing.

*All attendees and organizers at events affiliated with The AI Collective agree to our [privacy policy](https://www.aicollective.com/files/Data%20Privacy%20and%20Use%20Policy%20~%20The%20AI%20Collective.pdf) and are subject to our [code of conduct](https://www.aicollective.com/files/Code%20of%20Conduct%20~%20The%20AI%20Collective.pdf). Additionally, by registering for this event, you agree to The AI Collective Hampton Roads chapter [photo & video policy](https://sites.google.com/view/aicollectivehr/policies/photo-video).*

title: Kevin Feasel- Data Virtualization in SQL Server
description:
**IN PERSON MEETING!!!!!**
SQL Server's data virtualization stack has evolved throughout the past decade, allowing you to integrate SQL Server with a variety of external systems, such as Oracle, MongoDB, Cosmos DB, Azure Blob Storage, and AWS S3. In this talk, we will briefly cover the history of data virtualization in SQL Server and focus on how we can write T-SQL queries to access data from heterogeneous sources, both in SQL Server 2025 and in prior versions. Finally, we will look at several interesting use cases for data virtualization in the modern data environment.
group: Hampton Roads SQL Server User Group

title: Joey D'antoni- SQL Server 2025 Deep Dive: Essential Innovations for DBAs & Devs
description:
**IN PERSON MEETING!!!!!**
SQL Server 2025 is the AI database for enterprises. But there are more new things than just AI—in this demo filled, full-day workshop, you will learn about:

• Engine improvements that make everything just run faster
• How SQL Server 2025 helps you operate more securely
• Further enhancements to high availability
• Building event-driven architectures with change event streaming

Master the benefits of the latest release of SQL Server and how to deploy it in your organization.
group: Hampton Roads SQL Server User Group

\- Light\, optional conversation prompts to help break the ice if you want them
\- No presentations\, no panels\, no pressure to “network correctly”
\- A friendly\, welcoming environment for all backgrounds and experience levels
\- Come as you are\. Grab a cart or a basket\. Pick up dinner for the week\. \- \- Meet a few new people along the way\.

👥 **Who should attend**
Chats, Connections, & Grocery Carts is for:

People who enjoy meeting others without forced networking vibes
Builders, creatives, technologists, and curious minds
AI, automation, no-code, and tech-adjacent folks
Students and career switchers exploring what is next
Founders, consultants, operators, and professionals
Anyone who likes good conversation and low-stakes socializing

If you enjoy thoughtful chats and meeting people in unexpected places, you will fit right in.

🏪 **Why a grocery store**
You were probably going to the store anyway.

Hosting this event in a grocery store keeps things human, practical, and approachable. It removes the pressure of traditional networking and turns it into something simple, efficient, and a little fun.

Two errands. One cart.

💡 **Why this event exists**
Community grows best in relaxed, everyday spaces.

Chats, Connections, & Grocery Carts exists to create an easy, human-centered way for people to connect without titles, talks, or expectations. It is about building relationships, sharing ideas naturally, and strengthening the local community one conversation at a time.

Come say hi. Leave with groceries. Make a few new connections along the way.

**ℹ️ Other bits of info & FAQs**

* This is a free community event
* Drinks or food are available for purchase depending on the venue
* Photos or videos may be taken for community and marketing purposes
* Come solo or bring a friend
* ​​​Is there free parking in the area? Yes. They’ve got lots of spots available in their parking lot.
* ​​​Is this free? Yes, but space is limited. Make sure to RSVP.

**🤝 Organizers & partners**
**Organizers**
[The AI Collective Hampton Roads](https://linktr.ee/aicollectivehr)

**​Platinum partners**
[TechArk](https://gotechark.com/)
[Regent University](https://www.regent.edu/)
[ECPI University](https://www.ecpi.edu/)
[Hampton Roads Chamber Foundation](https://hrchamber.com/)

**Bronze partners**
[Assembly](https://www.assemblynfk.com/)
[757 Collab](https://757collab.org/)

**🌍 ​Our parent org**
[The AI Collective](https://www.aicollective.com/) is a global non-profit community uniting 150,000+ pioneers – founders, researchers, operators, and investors – exploring the frontier of AI in major tech hubs worldwide. Through events, workshops, and community-led research, we empower the AI ecosystem to collaboratively steer AI’s future toward trust, openness, and human flourishing.
